label: Outlook
description: Read, search, and send messages from a Microsoft Outlook or Microsoft 365 mailbox via Microsoft Graph.

form.title: Connect Outlook
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and send Outlook mail on your behalf via Microsoft Graph.

methods.label: Sign in with Microsoft (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: OAuth 2.0 via Microsoft Graph — a browser opens for you to grant Anton scoped access to your Outlook mailbox. Requires registering an Azure AD app and supplying your Client ID and Client Secret (steps below).
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with Microsoft (OAuth 2.0)

Anton uses your own Azure AD app registration (BYOK) to authenticate. You'll need a free Azure account to complete these steps.

## 1. Register an Azure AD app

1. Go to [Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → App registrations](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade) and click **New registration**.
2. Give it a name like `Anton Outlook`.
3. Under **Supported account types**, choose **Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts** (the `common` endpoint).
4. Under **Redirect URI**, select **Public client/native (mobile & desktop)** and enter `http://localhost` as the URI. Click **Register**.

## 2. Copy credentials

5. On the app **Overview** page, copy the **Application (client) ID** — paste it into **Client ID** below.
6. Go to **Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret**. Set an expiry, click **Add**, and immediately copy the **Value** — paste it into **Client secret** below. (It won't be shown again.)

## 3. Add API permissions

7. Go to **API permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Delegated permissions**.
8. Add: `Mail.ReadWrite`, `Mail.Send`, `User.Read`, `offline_access`.
9. Click **Grant admin consent** if your tenant requires it (optional for personal accounts).

## 4. Connect

10. Fill in the **Client ID** and **Client secret** fields below, then click **Submit**. A browser window will open for you to sign in with your Microsoft account and grant the requested permissions.

### Notes

- Client secrets expire. When yours does, generate a new one in Azure Portal and update it in Anton.
- To revoke access, visit [My Apps](https://myapps.microsoft.com/) → find the app → **Revoke permissions**.
- For single-tenant deployments, replace `common` in the auth/token URLs with your **Directory (tenant) ID**.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: Application (client) ID from your Azure AD app registration Overview page.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client secret
fields.description: The secret Value from Azure AD → Certificates & secrets. Shown only once at creation time.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID (optional)
fields.description: Leave as 'common' for personal or multi-tenant accounts. Set to your Directory (tenant) ID for single-tenant corporate deployments.

methods.label: IMAP / App password
methods.description: Connect via IMAP using your email address and a Microsoft app password. Works for personal Outlook.com and Hotmail accounts with two-step verification enabled.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via IMAP with an app password

This path works for **personal Outlook.com / Hotmail accounts**. Microsoft 365 (work/school) tenants typically require OAuth — check with your admin first.

## Prerequisites

- Two-step verification must be enabled on your Microsoft account.
- IMAP access must be allowed (it is by default for Outlook.com personal accounts).

## Steps

1. Sign in at [account.microsoft.com/security](https://account.microsoft.com/security).
2. Under **Advanced security options**, find **App passwords** and click **Create a new app password**.
3. Microsoft generates a 16-character password. Copy it immediately — it won't be shown again.
4. Enter your full Outlook email address in **Email** below.
5. Paste the app password into **App password** below.

## IMAP server details (for reference)

- **Server**: `outlook.office365.com`
- **Port**: 993 (SSL/TLS)

### Notes

- If you don't see the App passwords option, your account may have it disabled by an administrator. Use **Sign in with Microsoft** instead.
- App passwords bypass two-factor prompts — treat them like passwords and revoke any you no longer need.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Outlook email address
fields.description: Your full Microsoft / Outlook.com email address.

fields.name: app_password
fields.label: App password
fields.description: 16-character app password generated at account.microsoft.com/security. Not your regular account password.

fields.name: imap_host
fields.label: IMAP server
fields.description: Leave as default unless your organization uses a custom Exchange host.

fields.name: imap_port
fields.label: IMAP port
fields.description: Port used for the IMAP connection. 993 (SSL) is standard.
